summary: High‑energy 1983 performance with a stellar St Stephen, rare Wang Dang Doodle and China Doll; matrix mix captures the excitement.
review: The show is described as one of the top five hottest Dead concerts ever heard, driven by relentless energy and standout moments like Wang Dang Doodle, Jack Straw, Birdsong, Bertha, a rare Phil‑sung China Doll and an unforgettable St Stephen. Reviewers note no weak links and recommend a matrix or audience recording to fully experience the vibe, indicating excellent capture quality.

ai_rating.rationale: A single enthusiastic 5‑star review praises both performance and recording; strong show weight with many highlighted songs and a likely high‑quality matrix source leads to a high rating, but confidence is medium due to limited data.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer recommends matrix or audience recording as essential, implying the mix captures the live energy well; no complaints about clarity or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set strong with several highlights; second set ramps up even more, especially the pre‑drums run and rock‑in Bertha, maintaining relentless high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil sang lead on China Doll (rare at the time) using his own mic, marking a notable vocal contribution.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums run described as 'rockin', indicating a powerful drum buildup.
